author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Implemented v3 push/pull on branch `v3-push-pull` at commit `5c41d59b`
in `~/p/jjforge-v3-pushpull`. Push runs `git push <remote>
'refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*'`; pull runs `git fetch <remote>
'refs/jjf/*:refs/remotes/<remote>/jjf/*'` and applies the five-scenario
per-ref merge (copy / noop / fast-forward / merge-commit). The
diverged case calls `merge_ops::reduce_to_merged` on each parent's
loaded snapshot and lands a 2-parent commit whose tree carries the
LWW-resolved record + comments and whose message has a `Jjf-Op: merge`
trailer — so the v3 read path's `cat-file blob <ref>:issue.json` reads
the resolved snapshot at the tip.

New module `jjf_storage::sync_v3`. Free functions `push_v3_bare` /
`pull_v3_bare` let the fresh-clone case (no local sentinel yet)
transport without demanding a `Storage` handle. `Storage::is_v3()`
keys the CLI's v3-vs-v2 dispatch. Push/fetch refspec covers issues,
memories, and the meta sentinel — the meta ref's "copy" on scenario 1
is what bootstraps a fresh clone into v3-shape.

Took Option A on the test sweep: `JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION` is
gone from `push_pull.rs`; tests now call `jjf init` (lays the v3
sentinel) and exercise the v3 refspec. 15 tests, all green; full
workspace 460/460 green.

# Recommendation

Land as-is. Coverage in `crates/jjf/tests/push_pull.rs`: single-clone
round-trip, same-field LWW, different-fields union, different-issues
independent FF, fresh-clone sentinel bootstrap, clean-pull is FF-only,
unknown-remote / not-a-jj-repo / missing-init preflight.

# Confidence

High on push, scenarios 1-4 of pull, and the diverged-issue merge
(directly exercised by `pull_two_clones_same_field_lww_converges` and
`pull_two_clones_different_fields_both_survive`).

Medium on the memory-merge code path. Memories don't have a typed op
reducer like issues do; I picked the commit-timestamp-winning side and
landed a 2-parent commit. The path compiles and the workspace stays
green, but no integration test concurrently mutates the same memory
from two clones. Ticket 7's test sweep should add one.

# Open follow-ups

- The meta refs / `refs/jjf/meta/*` case for diverged-but-not-FF
  returns `Error::Invalid` rather than landing an undefined merge. In
  practice the sentinel never diverges (every writer plants the same
  trivial parentless commit content), but a different replica's
  bootstrap may produce a different sentinel oid. If that surfaces as
  a real operator pain point, add a "if both sentinels point at
  parentless trivial commits, pick local arbitrarily" branch in
  `merge_diverged_ref`.
- The v2 fallback path in `run_push` / `run_pull` is dead code in
  production (the migrator runs on every `Storage::open`). Ticket 7
  prunes it.
- `push_v3` / `pull_v3` on `Storage` returns `Error::Invalid` for v2
  mode; the bare free functions skip the mode check. Both surfaces
  exist for slightly different reasons (Storage callers want type
  safety; CLI wants bootstrap). Ticket 7 may consolidate.
- The `tempfile` dep would let `sync_v3` carry its own unit tests; I
  punted to integration-only coverage. Adding `tempfile` later is a
  one-line `Cargo.toml` change.
